title: Hydrodynamic Lubrication (Reynolds Equation)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Hydrodynamic Lubrication (Reynolds Equation): Reynolds equation inversion: recover bearing geometry, clearance, and lubricant properties from pressure measurements. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.reynolds.thin_film…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~100; viscosity_temperature_dependence, surface_roughness_effect set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: d/dx(h ** 3/eta * dp/dx) + d/dz(h ** 3/eta * dp/dz) = 6*U*dh/dx (Reynolds equation); p >= 0
E.world_state_x: film pressure field p(x,z) and bearing load capacity W
E.observation_y: pressure measurements p_i at N_sensors sensor locations in bearing

W.regime: Existence of the recovered pressure_film_field is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 100); viscosity_temperature_dependence dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Measurement gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: pressure field within 5% of exact for smooth surface. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~100. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |viscosity_temperature_dependence|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_grid_x * N_grid_z) per Reynolds solve per iteration
